description: Machine-readable inventory for legacy psmux, pmux, and tmux references after the binary alias removal gate.

entries.surface: legacy probe denial rules and tests
entries.reason: These references block old psmux startup probes and keep operators on winsmux startup checks.
entries.target: keep as stale-runbook denial examples while older local installs may still exist

entries.reason: tmux-compatible commands, configuration, targets, pane environment variables, and control mode remain supported product behavior after binary alias removal.
entries.target: keep unless a future product decision removes tmux-compatible behavior itself

entries.reason: The core runtime still implements tmux-compatible command, target, format, control-mode, and environment behavior.
entries.target: keep while tmux-compatible runtime behavior remains part of the product

entries.surface: existing .psmux state and setup lookup paths
entries.reason: Existing installs still use .psmux state and compatibility lookup paths during the sunset window.
entries.target: replace only after migration or explicit no-migration decision exists

entries.reason: The bridge exposes the legacy compatibility gate and still reads legacy review or role fields while current operator state can contain them.
entries.target: keep until operator state readers no longer need legacy field fallbacks and the gate is retired

entries.reason: The shipped Rust package no longer defines psmux, pmux, or tmux binary outputs. This entry keeps the completed sunset visible to the gate.
entries.target: keep removal_candidate_files at zero; rework any future matched file before v1.0.0 release

entries.surface: legacy upstream compatibility tests and diagnostics
entries.reason: TASK-407 keeps these as migration or reference material; they should not silently remain as active release blockers after replacement coverage exists.
entries.target: migrate, archive, or delete according to TASK-407 before v1.0.0

entries.reason: The gate must name the legacy terms it detects so future changes cannot add unclassified compatibility surfaces.
entries.target: keep while the repository still contains classified compatibility references
